response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer (15+ years) with genuine strengths in high-performance, event-driven backend systems, but the core of this role is deep, current C++/STL expertise in an industrial-automation context. His professional C++ exposure is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a C++ thesis project), with most recent work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message-broker platforms at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or OT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.), which are central requirements rather than nice-to-haves.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic handling millions of requests/sec
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, mentoring and workshop experience
Broad full-stack and event-driven architecture experience, including a self-built Kubernetes-deployable geo-data platform
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No demonstrated industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL) or OT/IoT protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
No Azure exposure and no background in automation machinery or industrial platform products

key_factors.factor: Insufficient hands-on C++/STL depth for a C++-centric role
No industrial-cybersecurity or OT protocol background
Strong high-performance, concurrent systems engineering fundamentals
